verdict_summary: Routine recompile of the Intel LPSS2 DesignWare I2C driver. All 10 WPP trace-provider GUID symbols were regenerated, accounting for the large number of changed functions. The two concrete instruction-level deltas (in ControllerDoReadPrepare and ControllerInitialize) are branchless-vs-branchy compiler codegen of logic that is bit-for-bit equivalent between builds. No behavioral or security change.

changed_functions.name: ControllerConfigureForTransfer
key_changes: Ordinary compiler codegen churn (register allocation, branch layout)
changed_functions.security_notes: Large function; the diff is consistent with module-wide WPP GUID rotation and recompilation. No concrete behavioral instruction-level change tying to a security fix was found.

changed_functions.name: ControllerInitialize
key_changes: RX FIFO threshold (IC_RX_TL, offset 0x38) selection rewritten from branchy cmp/jz/xor/mov to branchless dec/neg/sbb/and
Same value written for every input (0 when [dev+0xF0]==1, else 0xF)
changed_functions.security_notes: Unpatched (0x14000D5EC) selects IC_RX_TL via cmp dword [dev+0xF0],1 -> writes 0 if equal else 0xF. Patched (0x14000D738) computes the same result branchlessly (dec/neg/sbb/and 0Fh). The threshold is NOT hard-coded to 0 in the patched build; the value written is identical to the unpatched build for every input. Pure codegen difference.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED 0x14000D6C5: mov rcx,[rsi+38h]; add rcx,38h; cmp dword [rsi+0F0h],1; jnz L; xor edx,edx; jmp W; L: mov edx,0Fh; W: call Write.
PATCHED 0x14000D811: mov eax,[rsi+0F0h]; mov rcx,[rsi+38h]; dec eax; neg eax; sbb edx,edx; add rcx,38h; and edx,0Fh; call Write. Both: 0 if [dev+0xF0]==1 else 0xF.

changed_functions.name: ControllerProcessInterrupts
changed_functions.security_notes: Largest function in the driver. Diff consistent with recompilation and module-wide WPP GUID rotation. No concrete security-relevant behavioral change identified.

changed_functions.name: ControllerDoReadPrepare
key_changes: I2C read command selection (IC_DATA_CMD, offset 0x10) rewritten from cmovz to branchless neg/sbb/and/add
Same command word written for every input (0x500 READ|RESTART on first byte, 0x100 READ otherwise, plus 0x200 STOP on last byte)
changed_functions.security_notes: Unpatched (0x14000C940) composes the read command with test rdi,rdi / mov esi,100h / mov eax,500h / cmovz esi,eax, then bts esi,9 to add STOP on the last byte. Patched (0x14000CAB4) computes the same value with neg/sbb/and 0FFFFFC00h/add 500h, followed by the identical bts esi,9 STOP step. There is no missing-STOP path in either build: STOP (0x200) is asserted on the last byte identically. Register bit meanings are confirmed by the driver's own trace strings (0x200='| STOP', 0x400='| RESTART'). Pure codegen difference; the earlier missing-STOP / DMA-overrun claim was incorrect.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED 0x14000CAF1: test rdi,rdi; mov esi,100h; mov eax,500h; cmovz esi,eax; cmp byte [r13+78h],0; jz L; mov rax,r12; sub rax,rdi; cmp rax,1; jnz L; bts esi,9; L:... mov rcx,[r14+30h]; mov edx,esi; add rcx,10h; call Write.
PATCHED 0x14000CC57: mov rax,rdi; neg rax; sbb ecx,ecx; and ecx,0FFFFFC00h; add ecx,500h; mov esi,ecx; cmp byte [r13+78h],0; jz L; mov rax,r12; sub rax,rdi; cmp rax,1; jnz L; bts esi,9. Same command word both builds.

changed_functions.name: DmaInitSw
changed_functions.security_notes: Diff consistent with recompilation. No concrete security-relevant behavioral change identified.

changed_functions.name: FillDmaTxLliBuffer
changed_functions.security_notes: Diff consistent with recompilation. No concrete security-relevant behavioral change identified.

changed_functions.name: DmaTxComplete
changed_functions.security_notes: Diff consistent with recompilation. No concrete security-relevant behavioral change identified.

changed_functions.name: DmaRxComplete
changed_functions.security_notes: Diff consistent with recompilation. No concrete security-relevant behavioral change identified.

changed_functions.name: OnSequence
changed_functions.security_notes: SpbCx sequence callback (attacker-reachable entry point). Diff consistent with recompilation; no concrete parameter-validation or behavioral change identified.

changed_functions.name: OnCancel
changed_functions.security_notes: Request cancellation handler. Diff consistent with recompilation; no concrete race/lifetime behavioral change identified.

security_findings.prior_claim: Missing I2C STOP condition allowing DMA RX kernel pool overflow (medium).
security_findings.resolution: Not a vulnerability and not a change. Both builds issue the same per-byte read commands to IC_DATA_CMD (controller_base + 0x10): 0x500 (READ|RESTART) on the first byte, 0x100 (READ) on subsequent bytes, and 0x200 (STOP) on the last byte when [req+0x78] is set. The unpatched build selects the command with cmovz; the patched build with branchless neg/sbb/and/add. The STOP-on-last-byte step (bts esi,9) is present and identical in both builds. The prior report also swapped the DesignWare bit meanings: the driver's own trace strings show 0x200=STOP and 0x400=RESTART (not 0x400=STOP). There is no missing-STOP path and no reachable overrun.

security_findings.prior_claim: RX FIFO threshold changed from conditional 0xF/0 to unconditional 0 (medium).
security_findings.resolution: Not a change in written value. Both builds write 0 to IC_RX_TL (controller_base + 0x38) when [dev+0xF0]==1 and 0xF otherwise. The unpatched build expresses the select with cmp/jz/xor/mov; the patched build with branchless dec/neg/sbb/and. The threshold is NOT hard-coded to 0 in the patched build. Pure codegen difference with no behavioral effect.

summary: Diff between ialpss2i_i2c_bxt_p_unpatched.sys and ialpss2i_i2c_bxt_p_patched.sys is a routine recompile. All 10 WPP trace-provider GUID symbols were regenerated, which accounts for the 99 changed functions. The two concrete non-trace deltas both reduce to the compiler emitting a branchless computation in place of a branch: (1) ControllerDoReadPrepare (unpatched 0x14000C940 / patched 0x14000CAB4) composes the IC_DATA_CMD read command word identically in both builds (0x500 first byte, 0x100 subsequent, 0x200 STOP on the last byte); the earlier missing-STOP / DMA-overrun claim was incorrect and also swapped the STOP (0x200) and RESTART (0x400) bit meanings. (2) ControllerInitialize (unpatched 0x14000D5EC / patched 0x14000D738) writes the same IC_RX_TL threshold in both builds (0 if [dev+0xF0]==1 else 0xF); it is not hard-coded to 0. No function was added or removed. No security-relevant change is present.
